stem: A car salesperson earns a flat commission of $3.5\%$ on all sales. In one month they sold cars worth $\$42\,000$. Calculate their commission for the month.
An insurance agent earns a commission of $4\%$ on all policies sold. She sells policies totalling $\$65\,000$ in premiums this month. Calculate her commission.
A travel agent earns $2.5\%$ commission on the value of all bookings they process. Last month they processed bookings worth $\$180\,000$. What was their commission?
hint: Multiply the total bookings value by the commission rate expressed as a decimal.

stem: A business earns $6\%$ commission on advertising contracts it brokers. In March, it brokered contracts worth $\$28\,500$. Calculate the commission earned.

A salesperson earns commission on a sliding scale: $2\%$ on the first $\$20\,000$ of monthly sales and $4\%$ on any sales above $\$20\,000$. In April, they achieved sales of $\$35\,000$. Calculate their total commission.
hint: Calculate commission for each bracket separately, then add them together.

stem: A wholesale agent earns commission on a three-tier sliding scale: $3\%$ on the first $\$30\,000$, $5\%$ on the next $\$50\,000$, and $7\%$ on any sales above $\$80\,000$. Calculate the commission on monthly sales of $\$90\,000$.
hint: The three brackets are: up to $\$30\,000$; from $\$30\,001$ to $\$80\,000$; and above $\$80\,000$.

stem: A factory worker is paid $\$4.20$ per garment assembled. In one week they assemble $120$ garments. Calculate their weekly piecework pay.
hint: Piecework pay $=$ rate per item $\times$ number of items.

stem: A data-entry contractor is paid $\$2.80$ per completed form. In one week they process $350$ forms. What is their weekly income?
hint: Piecework pay $=$ rate per item $\times$ number of items.

stem: A sales consultant receives a monthly retainer of $\$1\,500$ plus $3\%$ commission on all monthly sales. In a typical month, they achieve sales of $\$45\,000$. A salaried position at a competitor pays $\$35\,000$ per year. By how many dollars does the salary exceed the annual commission-based income? Give your answer as a positive number.
hint: Calculate the typical monthly income, multiply by $12$ to get the annual income, then compare with $\$35\,000$.
explanation:
Monthly commission: $3\% \times \$45\,000 = \$1\,350$

Monthly income: $\$1\,500 + \$1\,350 = \$2\,850$

Annual income: $\$2\,850 \times 12 = \$34\,200$

Difference: $\$35\,000 - \$34\,200 = \$800$

The salaried position pays $\$800$ more per year.

stem: Renee receives a monthly retainer of $\$800$ plus sliding-scale commission: $2\%$ on the first $\$40\,000$ of monthly sales and $5\%$ on any sales above $\$40\,000$. Her typical monthly sales are $\$60\,000$. A salaried role pays $\$30\,000$ per year. By how many dollars does Renee's annual commission-based income exceed the salary?
hint: Calculate commission on each bracket, find total monthly income, then multiply by $12$ and compare.
explanation:
Commission on first $\$40\,000$: $2\% \times \$40\,000 = \$800$

Commission on remaining $\$20\,000$: $5\% \times \$20\,000 = \$1\,000$

Total commission: $\$800 + \$1\,000 = \$1\,800$

Monthly income: $\$800 + \$1\,800 = \$2\,600$

Annual income: $\$2\,600 \times 12 = \$31\,200$

Difference: $\$31\,200 - \$30\,000 = \$1\,200$

Renee earns $\$1\,200$ more per year on the commission arrangement.

stem: A sales representative receives an annual retainer of $\$24\,000$ plus sliding-scale commission: $1.5\%$ on the first $\$100\,000$ of annual sales and $3\%$ on any sales above $\$100\,000$. Their annual sales are $\$250\,000$. A salaried alternative pays $\$32\,000$ per year. By how many dollars does the salary exceed the commission-based total income?
hint: Calculate commission in two brackets, add the retainer, then find the difference from $\$32\,000$.
explanation:
Commission on first $\$100\,000$: $1.5\% \times \$100\,000 = \$1\,500$

Commission on remaining $\$150\,000$: $3\% \times \$150\,000 = \$4\,500$

Total commission: $\$1\,500 + \$4\,500 = \$6\,000$

Total annual income: $\$24\,000 + \$6\,000 = \$30\,000$

Difference: $\$32\,000 - \$30\,000 = \$2\,000$

The salaried position pays $\$2\,000$ more per year.

stem: Marco receives a monthly retainer of $\$2\,000$ plus $4\%$ commission on any monthly sales exceeding $\$25\,000$. His typical monthly sales are $\$70\,000$. A salaried position pays $\$44\,000$ per year. By how many dollars does Marco's annual commission-based income exceed the salary?
hint: Commission is calculated only on sales above $\$25\,000$.
explanation:
Sales above threshold: $\$70\,000 - \$25\,000 = \$45\,000$

Monthly commission: $4\% \times \$45\,000 = \$1\,800$

Monthly income: $\$2\,000 + \$1\,800 = \$3\,800$

Annual income: $\$3\,800 \times 12 = \$45\,600$

Difference: $\$45\,600 - \$44\,000 = \$1\,600$

Marco earns $\$1\,600$ more per year on the commission arrangement.
